q: Which Azure service reliably relays Dataverse messages to external systems via queues/topics?
why: Azure Service Bus queues/topics decouple and reliably deliver Dataverse messages to listeners.

q: Which Azure service is designed for high-throughput event streaming/telemetry ingestion?
why: Event Hubs ingests large-scale event streams; Service Bus is messaging; Event Grid routes discrete events.

q: Decide whether each Azure integration statement is true.

rows.s: Event Grid routes discrete events to handlers using a publish-subscribe model.
Logic Apps can orchestrate integrations similar to cloud flows.
API Management is used to store binary files.

why: Event Grid = pub/sub eventing; Logic Apps = orchestration; API Management is a gateway, not file storage.

q: When choosing an Azure integration option, what should drive the decision?
o: The messaging pattern and requirements (reliability, ordering, scale)
why: Pick the service by the integration pattern/requirements (reliable messaging vs eventing vs orchestration).

q: To publish Dataverse data to Service Bus, what must you register in Dataverse?
o: A Service Bus endpoint (service endpoint) and a step
why: You register a service endpoint pointing at Service Bus and a step to post messages on events.

q: What consumes the messages Dataverse posts to Service Bus?
o: A listener/consumer application reading the queue or subscription
why: An external listener reads the queue/subscription to process Dataverse messages.

q: Decide whether each Service Bus integration statement is true.

rows.s: Queues deliver each message to a single consumer.

why: Queues are point-to-point; topics with subscriptions enable pub/sub fan-out.

q: The Dataverse-to-Azure integration step posts the message at which point?
o: When the registered event (e.g. create/update) occurs
why: The step fires on the registered event, posting the message to the endpoint.

q: When should you use an Azure Function INSTEAD of a Dataverse plug-in?
o: For long-running, resource-heavy, or externally integrated logic
For simple pre-validation that must block a save
Never; plug-ins can do everything equally well
why: Functions suit long-running/heavy/external work; plug-ins have execution-time limits and run in-process.

q: A plug-in has a strict execution time limit. What is a common workaround for heavy processing?
why: Offloading to a Function (often via Service Bus) avoids the plug-in's time constraints.

q: Decide whether each Functions-vs-plug-ins statement is true.

rows.s: Plug-ins run within the Dataverse sandbox with time limits.
Azure Functions scale independently of Dataverse.
Azure Functions run inside the Dataverse event pipeline.

why: Plug-ins are sandboxed/time-limited; Functions scale separately and run in Azure, not the pipeline.

q: Which describes a good use of Azure with Dataverse for enterprise integration?
o: Publish events to Service Bus, process them in a Function
Store all UI in Event Hubs
why: A common pattern: Dataverse publishes to Service Bus and an Azure Function processes the messages.
